channel.title: Fetch includes parameters by default that cause empty results

item.title: Fetch includes parameters by default that cause empty results
item.dc:creator: adamsuskin
item.description:
Hello again community,

I’m using wp-source’s fetch action to try retrieve media at the endpoint /wp/v2/media. I have confirmed that accessing this directly via the browser returns results.

When I look at my network tab, I see that the request Frontity is shipping off is actually /wp/v2/media?_embed=true&slug=media. I don’t see this configured in my settings, and it causes empty results.

I’ve included my settings here for transparency. Is there any way to instruct Frontity to not include these parameters?

import { Packages } from "@adamsuskin/placeholder/types";
import { Settings } from "frontity/types";

const settings: Settings<Packages> = {
  "name": "placeholder",
  "state": {
    "frontity": {
      "url": "https://placeholder.com",
      "title": "placeholder",
      "description": ""
    }
  },
  "packages": [
    {
      "name": "@adamsuskin/placeholder",
      "state": {
        "theme": {
          "menu": [
            {
              "label": "placeholder",
              "link": "/placeholder"
            },
            {
              "label": "placeholder",
              "link": "/placeholder"
            },
            {
              "label": "placeholder",
              "link": "/placeholder"
            }
          ]
        },
      }
    },
    {
      "name": "@frontity/wp-source",
      "state": {
        "source": {
          "url": "https://wp.placeholder.com",
        }
      }
    },
    "@frontity/tiny-router",
    "@frontity/html2react"
  ]
};

export default settings;

Read full topic
item.source: Fetch includes parameters by default that cause empty results
